{{Quote|Without that treasure, Jabba'll soon have a price on our heads that'll make us the target of every bounty-hunter this side of Aquilae.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|<ref name="SW7">[[Star Wars (1977) 7|''Star Wars'' (1977) 7]]</ref>}}
'''Aquilae''' was an ancient [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] colony [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located along the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region.

==History==
Aquilae joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] early in its history, sometime between [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] and [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> The planet was discovered and colonized by the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]].<ref name="USGIL" />

{{Planet-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|But we can't turn back, fear is their greatest defense, I doubt if the actual security there is any greater than it was on Aquilae or Sullust and what there is is most likely directed towards a large-scale assault.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in response to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] defeatism (cut line from the script of ''Star Wars'')|<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref>}}
Han Solo's brief reference to Aquilae in [[Star Wars (1977) 7|''Star Wars'' (1977) 7]] suggests it is on the far side of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] from [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]].<ref name="SW7" />

Aquilae was one of the first planet-names seen in the early drafts of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], dating back to the [[The Star Wars story synopsis|first story treatment]]. In the shooting script, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] still referenced Aquilae, along with the planet [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], but this line was eventually cut out of the final movie.<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film" />

In the first story treatment, Aquilae, which is the Latin plural or genitive of ''aquila'', or "eagle", was a blue-green terrestrial planet threatened by a [[Space Fortress]] (later to become the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]). It contained the [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] [[Gordon]], and is the home of an anonymous [[Prince/Legends|princess]].<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film" />

In the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]], the princess was first named [[Leia (The Star Wars)|Leia]], and was the daughter of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Kayos]]. She succeeds to the throne of Aquilae at the conclusion of the script. It was home planet of [[Kane Starkiller]] and his sons [[Deak Starkiller|Deak]] and [[Annikin Starkiller]]. The planet was renamed [[Townowi]] in the [[The Star Wars first draft|first draft]]; by the [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second draft]] it only appears as in the name of the [[Aquillian Ranger/Legends|Aquillian Rangers]].<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film" />

[[Neva Kee/Legends|Neva Kee]] came from Aquilae in the revised [[Star Wars: Episode I The Beginning|rough draft]] of the script for [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="podracers">{{DB|organization|boontapodrace|Boonta Eve Classic Podracers}}</ref>
